explanations.1: This special comment tells Docker to use a newer syntax version that enables advanced features; it should be the very first line of the Dockerfile.
explanations.7: AS base creates a named intermediate stage that we can refer to later with --from, like saving a checkpoint we can build on top of.
explanations.13: Corepack is a tool bundled with Node.js that manages package managers like yarn; this enables it so yarn will work properly.
explanations.19: AS building_production names this stage so we can reference it later to copy files from this intermediate build without including everything in the final image.
explanations.22: The backslash at the end of this line lets us continue the same RUN command on the next line, keeping related steps together.
explanations.31: The asterisk in package.* is a wildcard that matches both package.json and package-lock.json (or yarn.lock), copying whichever lock file exists.
explanations.49: COPY --from=building_production pulls files from that earlier named stage instead of from your local machine, so we only copy the final build output.
